mnemonics: Friend Access: Friend functions are like VIP passes for operators, letting them directly access private members of a class from outside.
keyPoints: A friend function is not a member function of the class but has access to its private and protected members.
It is declared inside the class with the 'friend' keyword and defined outside the class like a normal function.
It is used for operator overloading when the left-hand operand is not an object of the class (e.g., cout << obj; or 5 + obj;).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare the function as a 'friend' inside the class, which is necessary for granting access.
Trying to define the friend function with the class scope resolution operator (::), as it is not a member function.
